evidence: Paper uses ranger package random forest on model residuals but never reports or interprets importance values. Results sections state only: 'Analysis of the residuals did not indicate additional modelling was necessary' (cases/hospitalizations) and 'Random forest regression on the residuals did not indicate any additional non-linear or interaction terms were necessary' (deaths). The Altmann p-values are used for a binary decision (need extra terms?), not for magnitude/ranking. All substantive conclusions derive from NB/Poisson regression coefficients: 'Incidence rate of COVID-19 increased between 1% and 4% per every unit increase of population density, enplanement and percent Black.'
